name: Baycrest Brain Health Center
description:
Getting approved for external care (physio, OT) is cumbersome and takes months... Yet the care provided in-house isn't consistent and not well organized and seems to inhibit progress in health condition. Our family feels that our family member would have made some more progress if it weren't for the delay in approval for the external providers and it's something to really consider when considering Baycrest for your family member. Though a lot of the nurses are wonderful and truly care.

Has improved a lot.  My mom has been here for 2 rehab stays.  One 4 years ago.  One recently.  She says it has improved a lot!  Her recent stay was a great, very pleasant experience.  No complaints on food.  Room is shared but spacious and clean.  Staff, specialist, doctors are all very friendly and helpful.  Thank you everyone at Baycrest for this.  Much appreciated

My mother is on 4th floor of the  apotex
Maybe because this floor cares for those with dementia
they don’t feel the need to have a warm presentation.
It’s dirty, neglected and short of staff.
Dirty floor a dusty old aquarium and what appears feces
on the cabinet.

My grandfather has been here after a long visit in the hospital and his condition has improved and his spirits. It’s a nicer facility than a hospital and the staff are friend.

Easy to reach with public transport. The hospital is quite big with multiple buildings. Each building encapsulating its own properties. I have been working here as a research student at Rotman Research Institute. I found all the people to be very helpful. Walking in the hostel and seeing happy faces of old people is what makes the aura awesome.

name: 101 Physio - Vaughan
address: 1520 Steeles Ave West, Unit 105, Vaughan, ON L4K 3B9
description:
I had my intake today at 101 Physio and the experience was fantastic from start to finish. The process was quick, easy to book, and everything was handled with care and professionalism. The staff were incredibly kind and helpful, making me feel comfortable right away. From the first phone call to walking through the door, everything was smooth and efficient. I truly appreciated how friendly and welcoming everyone was — it really set a positive tone for the rest of my care. Looking forward to continuing treatment with them!

I had a great experience with Physio101. They took me in as a new patient with no hesitation after a slip and fall incident back in June, and from day one, they've always taken care of me. Their scheduling is flexible and efficient, making it easy to stay consistent with my treatments. The staff are amazing—especially the secretaries, who are always friendly, helpful, and welcoming. The entire team is making my recovery process feel supported and stress-free. I would definitely recommend Physio101 to anyone dealing with injuries. You're in good hands here!

⭐ 1-Star Review for 101 Physio / Victoria Ginzburg

I am writing to share my very disappointing experience with Victoria Ginzburg, the owner of 101 Physio. I paid CAD $30,000 for an LMIA and job offer, which were later sold to Gamma Immigration Services (Master Visa). Unfortunately, my visa application was refused, and despite multiple requests, Victoria refused to return my money.

I worked very hard to earn this amount, and losing it has been a major financial and emotional setback. I trusted this process and expected honesty and professionalism, but I did not receive either.
I am sharing this review so that others are aware and take proper precautions before making any payments or agreements. Please make sure to verify all job offers, LMIA arrangements, and immigration partnerships

Highly recommend! My health is a priority and this is the trustworthy place to have your healing at!
I walked a dog and she saw bunny and pulled me real strong!!! And I came here for Chiro assessment and adjustment! I had great customer service with Halina and chiropractor Dr.Bill was professional and experienced. After just 1 treatment my body and neck felt so much better and he helped me to release pain that was bothering me.

101 physio has been my saving grace after my accident. Between helping manage my pain and providing me the proper treatment for my injuries and the at home feeling the staff incites I’m not sure which I appreciate more. I will most definitely continue to attend this clinic long after my injuries have healed. I highly recommend to anyone that needs help with their body. Thank you 101 physio !

name: 101 Physio - Scarborough
address: 8130 Sheppard Ave East, Unit 106, Scarborough, ON M1B 3W3
description:
I’ve been going to this clinic for a while and my experience has been the opposite of what’s described here. The staff have always been friendly, helpful, and accommodating. Like any medical office, sometimes there’s a short wait if a previous appointment runs over, but I’ve always been treated with respect and kept informed. The staff here are excellent and truly care about their patients

Very unsatisfied. The staff always seem moody and orders around. Even if you are here on time they will make you wait for a long time without explanation. Not recommended at all, there are much better physios out there.

The service here at 101 Physio Scarborough location has been amazing. They listen and do their best to accommodate and help you through whatever issue you are having. This experience they have given me has been truly appreciated. From the receptionist Nicole to the Doctors on site and the rest of the staff, they have all really helped with getting my quality of life back together.

The staff and receptionist are very friendly and caring. They listen and believe in helping you recover to your normal self. Always willing to recommend exercises to improve movements. Pick up and drop off service is great especially if recovering after a car accident.

Wonderful staff working here. My post accident recovery process is very smooth because of the way I’m treated. Special thanks to doctor Fatima, doctor Nishil and my favourite front desk person Nicole with her lovely voice.

name: Anderson Therapy Services
description:
Went for a psychoeducational assessment. Not great for girls, they are only looking for symptoms typical for males. The psychiatrist diagnosing my child never even met her and I was not told about this ahead of time.
The office staff, however, are very nice and helpful.

I can not say enough good things about our therapist at this location.  Due to my daughter being immune compromised we have been accessing their services virtually and Marina has been absolutely amazing.  She has been able to engage my daughter in handwriting in a fun and exciting way despite the challenges my daughter faces of ADHD and very poor fine motor coordination.  Marina is just fantastic.  She's able to keep her attention and has even made her love writing.  Thank you so much for the amazing work you do.

Denied entry into a speech therapy session for my son because I wouldn’t put a mask on. Please explain to me how speech therapy works with a mask on again?

Disappointing customer service and communication. Full transparency, I cannot speak to their services as their community manager didn't respond to my emails to book an appointment.

When my toddler wasn't talking at all, I started to panic until I found a speech therapist I could trust. I would highly recommend them.

name: Epilepsy Ontario
address: Unit 15 – 470 North Rivermede Road, Concord, ON L4K 3R8, Canada
description: Donate if you can, extremely worthwhile.

name: Clarington Retirement Home
description:
We the family of Ken Black would like to thank the staff of Seasons Clarington for being so good to our Dad.  He really enjoyed living at your residence these last nine years.  We were so grateful for you all for making his time there safe and happy.
As stated, he felt safe there and thankfully well respected.  We could not have found a better place for him to live.
The last year was very difficult as his health was declining.  We will always be grateful for the care and support shown to Dad and us.  The staff in all departments were absolutely amazing and we thank you for "going over and above" in caring for him.
We are also grateful to the many reside ts that we got to know and who expressed concern for him.  Your thoughtfulness and kindness was truly appreciated.
Anyone looking for assisted living for their parent(s) will find that Seasons Clarington is truly a place that they can feel comfortable and safe.  They have so much to offer.  The building is decorated tastefully and kept extremely clean.  There is plenty of activities for the residents to enjoy, the food is good and the staff is always ready with a smile.  The location is handy for shopping and banking, etc.  We also liked the fact that the building is a good size in that the staff actually has the time to get to know each resident.
Again, we are so grateful to all at Seasons Clarington for making our dad's life there a happy experience and for giving us the piece of mind that we set him up in such a wonderful place.
Trevor, Jan, Bill and Keith.

The food and residence was excellent at Clarington Place, but what was outstanding was the care and consideration from the staff. The kindness extended from top administration, to health care providers, daily staff attendants, and facility managers was constantly evident. I thank God that there are people like this working at Clarington.

My Mother spent four amazing years at Seasons!! The staff all knew her by name, and always treated her with care and respect. They provided great entertainment and activities for the residents to enjoy. The staff always reached out with any concerns or updates regarding her health.

The team at Seasons in Bowmanville is incredible. The staff is caring and extremely helpful. Kim, Sonia, Colleen and the entire team really helped my mom through her transition into the residence and for the rest of her life. The team have created a safe and friendly environment for everyone there. The meals are fantastic and the activities provide a wide range of interests. I'm really glad I was able to find the place and have my mom spend her final months of her life there. I can't thank the entire team there enough!

The staff at Seasons in Clarington are amazing.  They are kind, positive, encouraging and very professional.  They made my moms respite stay a very positive experience.  Thank you.

name: Seasons Amherstburg Retirement Home
description:
My father had a medical incident and was hospitalized for more than 2 months his condition varied from day to day even hour to hour surgery stabilizes him  He had a stroke in the hospital so life changed and he was sent to HTDG Tayfor for rehab. He was living alone unaided and now well we still didn't know. We had no idea what level of care he needed and what facilities could provide it, Seasons sent out a team to asses him and met with him . Memory care was what he now needed. we took a tour and liked the clean friendly environment. Staff were extremely helpful facilitating the move and helping us get him the care he needed. the food is excellent and varied and the staff keep the residents involved and having fun. Staff is very responsive to problems and concerns . Special thanks to Alesia at the front desk she is exceptionally cheerful and helpful .I feel very comfortable with my father in their care

I attended the Dementia Learning Series throughout March. Lana was excellent. She offered practical information and detailed tips that help make the whole process easier. Her knowlrdge, professionalism and genuine care for the attendees was much appreciated
Thank you!

The whole team at Seasons Memory Care floor were exceptional. I finally felt that my dad was in a safe, secure place  with people that truly cared and went the extra mile. Thank you so much for all you did for him  The time spent and your patience with him ( and me! 😊)

The facility is fabulous and clean with plenty of social opportunities and activities for the residents. The tour was very informative and we got to try the quality food and chat with many of the happy residents.

I have now lived at Seasons Amherstburg for 3 months and I couldn’t be happier. It was a huge life change for me and I was very worried at first that it might not be the right decision.
I’ve made friends and been on many adventures I would not have done when I was living alone in my home. The staff is very accommodating and special thank you, to Yosef our bus driver who goes out of his way to ensure we are safe and have fun on our adventures!
I would highly recommend Seasons Amherstburg, come for a tour and talk to the residents and see for yourself how happy we are!
J. Thomson

name: Seasons Belle River Retirement Home
description:
Tour was very thorough. Jeremy took the time to provide alot of valuable information. Beautiful Retirement Home.

First of all, thank you for giving Jacki and I the opportunity to provide our thoughts on being a resident of Seasons Belle River.

Let's just say at the start we are, if you'll pardon the old expression "Over the moon" with our experience with your staff, Facilities and care.

Jacki and I were First introduced to a wonderful lady named Tia, who, walked us through the Seasons experience. If there is a way Seasons could clone her it would be a great thing for Seasons to do.

After our initial meeting we were left with no doubt that moving into Seasons was the best way Jacki and I could spend the autumn of our lives in dignity and in great care.

We eventually met the other members of the seasons team, including Tara, Lyndsay, Mike, Mykaela, nurses, kitchen staff and many others.

The staff here obviously have been trained in making sure every residents needs are met and they do it with such care and empathy. We don't know who does the training but their secret recipe should be bottled for others to follow.

Our home, the meals, and our care have been nothing but First class.

If you are looking for recommendations I would say, don't change a thing.

You may feel that you are glad in securing another resident, but trust me, Jacki and I are the lucky ones.

Thanks again for letting Jacki and I share our thoughts with you. we are very proud to be part of the Seasons Family.

Yours, Jacki and Ted Burdette

Sara provided us with a very informative tour of Seasons Retirement.  We were very impressed with what we saw and she was able to answer all our questions.  The tour has greatly assisted us in making a decision on a retirement home, when the time comes.

I've been visiting my grand father here over the last few days. As I live far away, this has been my first opportunity to do so since he moved here about a year ago. The facilities are top notch. Spacious, clean and well maintained. There's a theater room, a chapel, a very nice dining room, a cafe, a community garden for residents and beatifully landscaped courtyards, as well as several areas with bookshelves or board games for residents to gather in. Most importantly, the staff is wonderfull. They are friendly and courteous it's clear they care about their work and the residents under their care. It's been a great comfort knowing my grandfather is in good hands.

I am so grateful to the staff at Seasons for everything they do for my father. Their thoughtfulness, attention, patience, compassion and communicative joy are touching. Furthermore the beautifully maintained building and multitude of activities make Seasons a place where my father is happy and active. I could not wish for a better place for him.

name: Seasons Cambridge Retirement Home
description:
My mother really enjoyed her time at Seasons. It is a beautiful home, very clean and bright and located on a quiet street. It has a nice atmosphere. The staff are very friendly, helpful and compassionate. There are many activities throughout the week for everyone to enjoy and outings to participate in. There are two walking tracks, indoor and out and a beautiful seating area to enjoy outside.
I am very grateful my mother had such a wonderful place to spend her last years.

Erica was wonderful, she is very compassionate and personable. She knew exactly how to interact with my hesitant father.  By the  time the tour ended  my father was converted from hesitant to excited to move in asap.
not only are their facilities and suites beautiful.  All of the staff we met also exuded a gentle kindness about them.

What an amazing place! We are thoroughly impressed everytime we visit our mother at Season's Retirement Home in Cambridge. The facility is so beautiful and the staff is always so happy and helpful! Most recently, we visited early Sunday morning (7:30 a.m.) and decided to sit down stairs and enjoy the complementary Bistro. It was so bright, clean and beautiful!! The bistro was fully stocked with fresh bananas, apples, oranges, muffins, croissants, coffee, tea and juices. For a moment, we felt like we were on a holidays..lol!!

We recently toured Seasons Retirement Home in Cambridge and had the pleasure of meeting Erica. From the moment we arrived, she was warm, welcoming, and incredibly knowledgeable. Erica took the time to answer all of our questions with patience and care, making us feel comfortable every step of the way.

The tour was thorough and informative, and Erica highlighted all the wonderful amenities and programs that make Seasons feel like such a vibrant and caring community. Her professionalism and genuine kindness stood out and made the experience enjoyable.

Thank you, Erica, for making our visit such a positive one. We left feeling very reassured and impressed with both you and Seasons Cambridge.

It was a very constructive experience meeting & touring with Erica.  She was most knowledgeable and patient as  we met and discussed many questions.  This was followed by an extensive tour of the various areas in the building including a viewing of both a one bedroom & two bedroom apartment.    My wife and I both concluded half way through the tour that this was the place for us!  Erica is a most positive Leasing Manager for Seasons.  -- Bryan & Diane

name: Seasons London Retirement Home
description:
I have toured Seasons London 2x.  Once on my own and once with my sister and mother.  This is the one place my mother had wanted to live, if they had to move after living in the home they built 70 yrs ago.

Sarah was absolutely wonderful.  She made our experience so warm, wonderful, and welcoming.  Sarah was patient and, very informative and was able to answer all of our questions. She questioned us on our parents needs and wants.  Sarah came through with so much knowledge.  She was able to accommodate us with a home where we were hoping for in the building.  Everyone seemed so happy there. The employees all had smiles, the residents were willing to give us feed back with our questions as well.  Some have just moved in, and were adjusting still, some have been here, from the opening.  Seasons is bright, beautiful, well layed out, so much to offer.  No matter when you make such a move, there will be adjustments.  This building has a ton of things to do, to help with this and to meet new friends.

We were very impressed with the opportunity to complete the full tour at Seasons. The staff we met were obviously very impressed with the organization, the accommodations, and the extras provided, and so were we. We would like to come back for another mini tour as we have lots of questions about how Seasons can meet all of our needs. Should be exciting!

Sarah represented Seasons wonderfully as she manned the tent and supplied water and pop to 160 seniors at the Huff n' Puff's Annual Picnic. Thank you so very much! You're amazing!

The Staff at Seasons Retirement Community are all wonderful! So upbeat kind and friendly! We felt very welcomed! Sarah was great in giving us all the answers to the many questions we had - so patient! The facility is bright, open, clean and beautiful!! The first place of many that my mom absolutely loved!

I am so grateful to one of the staff members here. I was driving to work today when my vehicle told me I had a flat tire. I pulled into the parking lot. After calling several people in a panic, I decided that I will try and change the tire. One of the staff members, who just parked, noticed I had a flat and offered right away to pump it up. It took 20 minutes to do it and I am so grateful that they helped me out. I was afraid that she would be late for work. I did call the front desk to let them know and to thank the staff member for me again, when I was at Jiffy Lube. But this experience made me breathe so much easier knowing someone was able to help me, especially on this cold morning (-8 Celsius) I wished I asked for her name though. My apologies. Thank you so much!! 💜

name: Seasons Milton Retirement Home
description:
Jenee is absolutely fabulous!  Such a positive attitude and great enthusiasm for her job.  She is so helpful and knowledgeable, answered my many questions, and gave me confidence that Seasons is the right place for my mom.

The staff are truly fabulous – kind, thoughtful, and compassionate. They go above and beyond to care for the residents, making them feel like family. They know everyone by name and always offer words of encouragement and comfort.

My grandmother moved into Seasons Retirement Homes in May of this year after leaving the home she lived in for nearly 40 years. We were all a bit nervous about how she'd adjust, but to our surprise, she’s done incredibly well — and she absolutely loves it!

The entire family is in Milton, so I visit regularly, and every time I go, I’m struck by how warm and welcoming the environment is.  Everyone there seems genuinely happy — both residents and team members — and it really shows in the atmosphere.

We honestly never thought my grandma would settle in so comfortably, but she has. We are so happy to have her close to us and happy.

This is a huge shout-out to Sarah, Prab and all the wonderful staff at Seasons. My mother entered the home mid summer after some troubling health concerns, but with the staffs help and the fantastic facilities, she is doing better than ever! We are extremely happy with the friendly staff and management and would absolutely recommend Seasons.

Friendly and welcoming staff, beautiful facility

name: Seasons Owen Sound Retirement Home
description:
Our parents moved into Season's Owen Sound just over one year ago. We could not have picked a better place. The home is kept in top condition. It's clean and inviting. The staff is second to none. Right from the kitchen, cleaning, nursing and all the support staff. Polly the manager works hard to make sure all your needs are meant. I have emailed her at all times, and within minutes I have a reply. I can't say enough about this senior retirement home. The pictures below is to show the extra effort to made to make their guests more at home.

My wife and I are not (quite) ready to move, but we are beginning to look for a retirement residence. We were received warmly and made to feel very welcome by Jeff and other staff. Our tour started with an (entirely unexpected) very good complementary lunch. All of our questions were answered thoroughly and thoughtfully during the tour after lunch and at the final 15-minute meeting in Jeff's office. Many, many thanks!

Anyone with an elderly parent that you love very much knows how important it is to find a facility that will provide an environment where they can continue to be loved and supported 24/7.  Even more challenging to find a place that can provide the level of nursing assistance to deal with dementia diagnosis and disease progression.

Fortunately in Owen Sound Ontario, right here in Grey County I found this facility.

My mom moved into Season's on December 2nd. Words cannot describe the sense of relief and peace that is experienced as I visit my mom and see the smiles, and hear the laughter that has been silent for a while.

Keep up the great work Staff & Team at Season's in Owen Sound..special thanks to Polly, Jeff & Breanna for your leadership and assistance during my mom's transition.

Jeff Elford

Jeff created a welcoming visit. We were able to sit with dad through a lunch.  Jeff introduced us to the available suites and we appreciated observing how staff interacted with residents. We like that each suit has a balcony, the gardens and activities seem extensive and are good.

Probably one of the best experiences I’ve had dealing with a parent who requires assisted living. The staff, “especially Jeff” could not have done more to accommodate and adapt to my families situation. “Thumbs up all round”.

name: Seasons Royal Oak Village Retirement Home
description:
Sincere Appreciation to the entire staff at Season's Royal Oak Village Retirement Home.
I've just completed my placement there and it was such an amazing time. The staff are extremely welcoming, and encouraging. The amount of activities and outings that were planned for the residents was amazing, I did not know retirement homes did that many activities. Which I then found out later they do not. This retirement offers that many activities and outings all thanks to the Fun Manager Karyn Anderson, and the staff that helps facilitate Karyn and participate in the activities.
Karyn was not only the Fun Manager that organized many activities such as Bean bag toss (cornhole) residents against staff or the weekly happy hour with live music held Friday afternoons but she also overseas the students allowing us to learn and experience in a retirement setting. Thank you Karyn.

I've done many placements/ clinicals as a Nursing student and now PSW student and this placement has been by far one of my favorites. Not only are the staff amazing, and the residents extremely welcoming, but the communication and organization in this home is mind blowing. The staff hold meetings everyday that allows everyone to be up to speed with the activities and outings of the day, the meals of the day, residents that will be out for the day, any updates with any residents. This is something I was soo happy to be apart of. With these meetings being held ALL the staff are aware of everything they would need to know to help give the best care they can provide.
I am soo thankful for the opportunity to have been apart of this great team.
Thank you Seasons Royal Oak Village Retirement Home!

Arrived to visit a family friend.   Staff was very polite and helpful.....BUT....the overpowering perfume scent from reception lady was giving me a headache.   I thought these were scentfree environments.   Due to allergies  it is unlikely I will be able to visit there again.

Tammy and the team at Seasons are incredibly welcoming and fantastic people. We toured the property and felt at ease the entire time. We are considering this place for our parents' next phase in life, and this could be the right fit! I highly recommend not only speaking with Tammy, but meeting her on site.

Tammy was amazing, she showed us the entire facility, answered every question and was so kind.  Tammy is very proud of her retirement community and the work she does and it showed in her positive, compassionate interaction with my mother.

My Mother was a resident on the Memory floor, although it was not a long stay.  Tammy was very helpful throughout the process of setting up and moving into the home.  Katie and her staff are terrific and very caring.  I would highly recomment this facility to others.

name: Seasons St. Catharines Retirement Home
description:
Update: Frances (the manager) met with me to discuss my father's concerns. She was really great, honestly. Frances resolved everything she could for him and her time and effort is very appreciated. My Dad ended up having to install his own internet and he still chooses to skip most dinners because the food is typically quite horrible but he's happy otherwise!

This was my second tour of Seasons with Machaella, from sales, the second being with my sister. We are looking for a place for my father, and we have found it at Seasons.

I want to find a retirement home for my mom in law and made an appointment to visit on Wednesday Dec 3rd,  but when I want to change a date to come  on Dec 2th around 7:15 pm and called the number on website, the lady who picked up the phone is strange, the only thing she speaking is she didn’t understand what I am talking about and hang up the phone quickly. Thank you for picking up the phone!

What a first-class facility. I am looking for an independent senior facility and I went to Seasons on Ontario Street St Catharines. I was escorted throughout the facility by Matt who took the time and effort to show me throughout and point out all benefits and expenses. He even went to the maintenance crew to get answers regarding 110 volt electric plugs. He's very professional and I appreciated the job that he did.

I toured Seasons with my stepfather. It was a great experience, it's a beautiful facility, the staff is super knowledgeable and makes you feel at ease. I looks like a wonderful community.

name: Seasons St. Thomas Retirement Home
description:
We have toured seasons on two separate occasions to get an idea if it would be a good fit for my grandmother.  Emma Grant is super sweet and answered all of our questions.  She is really great with the seniors. She made my grandmother comfortable with the idea of moving.  The manager of the kitchen also introduced himself and said how he would be happy to accommodate a vegetarian diet. All of the staff are very nice and we do plan on sending her here once she is ready .They have many different activities such as knitting and live music.  Seasons is a great place ,a much better alternative to your traditional government ran nursing homes.

My tour and impression of the facility was very positive.  People were friendly, the atmosphere was very calm.  Emma was very open and honest when she answered my questions.

Very friendly & knowledgeable. Lovely newish assisted living facility, which feels more like a luxury hotel! We feel privileged that Mom can have this choice, financially, and not have to stay at home, at risk, while waiting for a "regular" LTC bed.

Relaxed atmosphere. Upscale decor, Has a mini grand piano in one of the lounge areas on the main has a theater room, workout room, VON exercise classes, a studio, dog park, and large beautiful patio on the north side.

Resident service is excellent and personalized

It was a true pleasure dealing Emma Grant! She was extremely helpful, friendly, knowledgeable, and professional! I will definitely be moving my mother to the Seasons Retirement Community!

name: Seasons Strathroy Retirement Home
description:
A very lovely retirement facility, wonderful and friendly staff, spacious, bright, well kept .

Amazing views from my grandfathers room.  It is located just far enough out of town that you can only see the countryside , including a family of deer on the grounds!  Very much recommend for long term residence!!

Great place! Great people! Very good vibe from the moment you walk in.

Tour was well organized. After seeing all Season's had to offer we were treated to a nice lunch

Loved seeing the staff interact with the residents. And they knew everyone.

name: Seasons Welland Retirement Home
description:
Michaella Kallie’s, was very welcoming and knowledgeable. We enjoyed our tour very much. Olivia also was very attentive and welcoming. She was very helpful and patient when explaining the paperwork. All in all both ladies were outstanding.

Sabrina gave my father and I am excellent your of the property and all the resources.  She was very professional, friendly and understanding of my father's position.  The residence is beautiful and 5star to all the others my father has toured.  I would recommend this residence to anyone looking for a place for their loved ones.

My mother has been a resident in memory care at Season's Welland for just over 6 months now.  She had been in another memory care facility prior to this which we were not happy with. After having such a bad experience elsewhere I have been diligent in making sure mom is being taken care of well.

Guess what? The staff here is absolutely top notch! The thing I'm happiest with is the communication between family and the MC Manager Tara and her staff. From emails that provide me with glimpses of mom's week or phone calls I make with questions that need a speedy answer, these people are doing a great job of keeping me informed about my loved one. I know my mother is in good hands.

From the first tour almost a year before her move in to present day, I would highly recommend Season's Welland (and have numerous times).   On top of that we are saving almost $2000 a month compared to the other place. Thank you Larry, Dale and everyone there - you do make a difference! Thank you to everyone who works directly with mom providing her with what she needs and peace of mind for our family.

Sabrina was very thorough with her information and the tour on site. She provided plenty of time for questions or clarification. Receptionist and staff we encountered were friendly and made us feel welcome. Plenty of information to take home and consider along with an open invitation to attend upcoming events. Excellent introduction to your residence!

We could not have felt more welcome at Seasons. Everyone was helpful and we couldn't expect more than that. Our meetings with Anne Marie and Michaella were wonderful and made us feel right at home.  Thank you to all.

name: Seasons Corporate Office
description:
My sister's and I are looking for the best place to have our mother live at and Season Retirement home is the perfect place to be we loved our tour with Jeremy he was very professional and very happy to answer all our questions. The apartment are beautiful well laid out. We would definitely recommend Season Retirement home to all who are looking for a fun place to live. We think this would be the perfect fit for our mother but unfortunately my mother is not ready yet. Thank you Jeremy for your time and kindness.

A very positive and welcoming environment. Went for a tour and was greeted by all the staff as if we had known each other for years. Made us feel like home.

Excellent tour, all questions were answered.
The property feels like a home and all of the spaces have been thoughtfully planned.  All of the staff we have interacted with have been wonderful.

My mother and I really enjoyed our tour. The facility is lovely, and Paul was very patient and informative.

Very disappointed with the lack of consistent care, rooms not cleaned properly or regularly. Huge staff turnover.  Memory care dining is awful.

name: Ontario Health atHome — North Bay Office
address: 1164 Devonshire Ave., North Bay, ON, P1B 6X7
description:
Shouldn't even have one star. If you love your loved ones, do not recommend.

They have done an wonderful job for my husband and I. Not easy when they are understaffef!

name: Ontario Health atHome — Parry Sound Office (By Appointment Only)
address: 6 Albert Street, Parry Sound, ON, P2A 3A4
description:
Highly professional doctors and nurses who took care of me when I had an infection from a laminatomy. They were compassionate ,efficient and listened to my concerns . I spent 9 days in acute care and received excellent care from the nurses as well. Watching the doctors and nurses in action gave me time to understand longer wait times in ER. Nurse are literally pulled from patient care duties to assist in ER when necessary.Thank your care and compassion during my recovery.

My father had emergency surgery at the hospital. He appreciated the quick, attentive, thorough service from the DI department, LAB, ER, operating room staff, and recovery. Even in the code gridlock they where still able to find a room for him for recovery. He was beyond grateful.

The surgeons Dr Witt and Dr Champion where easy to talk to and helped alleviated his stress about the surgery. They are exceptional surgeons. The anesthesiologist was amazing aswell. The services there were phenomenal.

Hospital is so incredibly slow. Went to emerg yesterday at 9am didnt leave till 5pm. Was told to come back today for an ultrasound as per the docs orders. He told us go to the main doors we did. 2:45pm Went to main reception they didnt have it booked. Was sent to the techs at xray/ultrasound they didnt have it booked. Got sent to emerg had to wait for triage to get it booked. Waited another hour got the ultrasound. after we mentioned a new symptom and was told a nurse would come consult shortly, never did (3 hours ago). its now 830 and we still havent seen a doc to consult with us on the ultrasound.

The only reason this isnt 1 star is because I have never had an issue with the staff they have always been super nice.

ICU unit, fantastic nurses and Doctors! I was in waiting for a pacemaker. I can't say enough about the staff that looked after me last week. Friendly caring and informative. Made me fill at ease . It's not a hospital that's makes it  great. It the fantastic Nurses and Dr. That work there! Love u guys in the ICU ! Merry Christmas and all the best to all of you in the New Year!
Paul Abendroth. 😊

Although I have nothing good to say about the current state of our healthcare system as a whole, our frontline workers are absolute heroes in my opinion. One such hero is Andrew Moore, RN at West Parry Sound Health Centre.
After already spending 3 hours in the waiting room waiting to be seen for a medical issue that I deemed to be important, only to be told that it could still be a couple more hours before the doctor would see me, in tears I told the emergency room receptionist to take my name off the list and that I was leaving. It was at this point the receptionist asked whether I wanted to at least talk to a nurse about my issue before I left. I said yes and then Andrew came out to the waiting area and, right there and then, examined me to advise that my badly swollen calf and ankle did not appear to be a serious issue such as a blood clot, and at least I was able to leave with some peace of mind if not a medical explanation of why this sudden swelling occurred.
So thank you Andrew - your kindness and compassion exhibited in a time of great stress for me meant the world. You are truly an asset to West Parry Sound Heath Centre!  I only wish there were more people like you out there to make this world a better place!

name: Ontario Health atHome — Sault Ste. Marie Office
address: 390 Bay Street, Second Floor, Sault Ste. Marie, ON, P6A 1X2
description: Colleen always takes care of us and our loved ones

name: Ontario Health atHome — Sudbury Office
address: 40 Elm St, Suite 41-C, Sudbury, ON, P3C 1S8
description:
Excellent care by Nurse Practitioner Sandra today at the Public Health Centre. This is the closest location to Lavigne, ON for a woman to get her pap test. The staff was friendly and professional at check in and Sandra's bedside manner made an always uncomfortable appointment comfortable.

All of these reviews are incorrect. I have been to the sexual clinic several times, and they've always been amazing to me. I have gone in for physicals and STI testing, and they have been amazing.

I prefer to do any type of STI testing at a public health compared to a walk-in. They are incredibly professional here.

Called to ask what my options were for an IUD removal. Was called back and the nurse on call for the "sexual health" department on 23/09/21 in the morning was extremely unhelpful and unprofessional. She had no suggestions or solutions just oh well you have a doctor, figure it out. I mentioned that it was uncomfortable and past due and nothing just was told its fine if it goes past due. I asked again as it was referred for me to call them as it was inserted at the hospital and I would feel more comfortable with a female. Still no help. The call left me more upset and embarrassed that it should have. Shame on you public health.

Services and advice should be given without judgements and bias. Easy and simple access to care. We women need more support....

Not a good experience calling to inquire about my options. Asking for help to find dental work as I’m a student on a budget with no dental insurance. Was met with a snappy “well we don’t treat your age group at all” and then a quick transfer to a different number where I was put directly to voicemail. No advice or empathy.

I do not recommend this clinic if you are wanting to access harm reduction supplies. The workers can be very stigmatizing and don't actually understand the core concepts of the harm reduction philosophy/movement: compassion, empowerment, etc. Please access Reseau access or SACY for harm reduction supplies instead, they will acknowledge you and treat you as a person. One of the nurses/staff at this clinic won't even look at you or acknowledge you when you walk into the room. Public Health has
no business standing behind our movement and claiming 'harm reduction' if they treat people this way. You have a human right to access good healthcare and maintain an adequate standard of life for your well being - no matter what! Get harm reduction supplies/services from peers whom hold life experience and want the best for you, however you decide that is, and don't just see you as a number.

name: Ontario Health atHome — Timmins Office
address: 330 Second Avenue, Suite 101, Timmins, ON, P4N 8A4
description:
Couldn't ask for a more caring team of people who take the time to answer questions and follow up with the care of our loved ones.

Excellent

name: Ontario Health atHome — London Office
address: 356 Oxford Street West, London, ON, N6H 1T3
description:
Not sure what others are going on about, but they have been extremely kind when I've called due to issues with the VON (as ON Health At Home is just there to facilitate you getting connected with other care organizations).  They were able to expedite delivery of medical supplies and contact and call all sorts of folks - always returned calls when promised and did everything they could, in a limited system, to get us the care we needed.

My mother was discharged from the hospital over a week ago, and we were told someone from Ontario Health at Home would be in touch. No one has contacted us. We’ve been trying to reach her caseworker repeatedly but have only gotten voicemail, with no call back. While we understand it’s summer and staffing may be limited, our family crisis hasn’t paused. We’re doing everything we can to set our mother up for a safe and healthy recovery, but the lack of support is making that nearly impossible. This is incredibly frustrating and disheartening, especially when timely care could prevent another hospital admission.

Dad discharged from hospital with orders to be seen at home daily. I call to initiate service and get obstacle after obstacle. Didn't come for 2 days. Coordinator suggested we pay for private care or do you know a nurse who can help you out. Tells me all set up. No call or visit from nurse. I call and somehow they didn't send me to next step to give von my info. Von calls apologizing and sets up nurse. Nurse doesn't show up. They called his house phone even though right on discharge papers and every step of the way I gave my phone# and address as he's staying with me. Von sets up scheduled days nurse doesn't come. They call his house again  said they don't have my # even though they called .e earlier in the day usu g that#

The everyday psw’s are great , the long time employed ones suck , cons , Bayshore Manager is rude and disrespectful to clients , who are recovering from surgery 😡 I plan to contact the Ministry

They force "environmental visits" even during covid even though they were already at your home 2 times in previous month's and make it so you don't have a choice if you want your nursing hours you have to comply other wise it's held over your head! and done against your will They don't care about potentially exposing medically fragile people in the least my loved one ends up on a ventilator and in the critical care unit every time they are sick they have knowledge of this and don't respect this fact it's ironic. they work at home to protect themselves and their families but don't give you the choice to protect yours!!!Also they breach privacy and confidentiality of the patient and yourself as the caregiver and there are NO repercussions.
