name: Davenport-Perth Neighbourhood and Community Health Centre
description:
The center hast programs for all ages. The literacy program is great. The general staff and department heads are caring. hard working,  dedicated and helpful  The center needs to train most of  their volunteers to be pleasant with participants. A usual female volunteer has a cold , unpleasant and sad face at all times. Peter a new  staff member is a great addition to the team.The counseling department needs improving.It took a lot of effort to get an appointment with confusing and wrong information given about securing an ntiial appointment I attended f4 session out of 20  there with Debbie Tripp and found the experience unsettling  and   she just did not seem to not want to get involved.I canceled the rest of the appointments. A very surface individual.  Only because of this experience I give it a 3 otherwise it would rate higher.

I've had weekly classes for a few years now. I've learned so much from the computer literacy class.

Wanda Tse has been especially amazing. She has helped me with a variety of subjects including helping me with problem  solving and everyday issues that come up.

This review is due many years
I and my family are patients at this clinic for 11years, back on 2018 I was for 5 months trying to find what my daugther's illness was I went several times to many hospitals and all I found was rolling eyes and doctors that didn't really listen, call Davenport health centre and Helena answer the phone, every one in that front desk are amazing I only have great experiences with all of them, but Helena since day one has a special place in my heart, so after explaining what was happening,  my daugther's doctor was not available  but she was able to get my daugther an appointment with Dr Arruda.
As soon as we were called and still on the hallway Dr Arruda look at my daugther and said she didn't like her color and hiw bruised she was and I started explaining that I have been to may hospitals and no one listens she stoped me and ask what I wanted to do, THANK YOU!!
Long story short, she knew and I knew too, she wrote a letter and sent me rigth away to Sickkids hospital, my daugther had cancer, leukemia and like the oncology team assure me she would have just a couple days left if Dr Arruda didn't sent me there.
Helena and Dr Arruda both of you saved my daugther's life I will always be in debt to both of you, there's not enough money in this World that pays what you have done and still do.
We could think it was a "lucky guess" with my daugther but for the past I think 3/4 years Dr Arruda is my husband's doctor and she is really an amazing and caring doctor.
My daugther's cancer relapsed 2y ago and she is currently under treatment again at sickkids hospital, but it eases my heart knowing I can always count on Davenporth health centre.

Was a patient since they opened.... When there was no medical building, no community services, no daycare, and no pharmacy.  The only thing I noticed is the consistent change from Drs, to nurses, to nurse practitioners....  I watched a lot of those attending to me study through school and neglect my health issues. Even reading books for school during Mt appointments.  From being treated like a  patient, to being treated like a number.  No follow ups.  Lack of courtesy, respect,  and professionalism from the senior secretary has contributed to my choice to find another Dr. Tired of being unable to speak with a doctor, waiting for an hour plus when I arrive on time for appointments. Tired of hearing go somewhere else when unable to book an appointment. Most importantly, tired of being hung up on when trying to speak to my Dr.  So, I did.  I went elsewhere.  I hear a lot of people saying they are satisfied with the medical services at this location, it's because they are new to this facility.

I look forward every Thursday to have my appointment with Wanda Tse.

She is the Progam Supervisor of Adult Literacy Services

She has helped me tremendously with my basic computer skills. As well as everyday tech issues.

name: Scarborough Inter-Professional Primary Care (IPPC) Team
description:
Best cardiologist in town
Dr. Derek yung and etc.

To find the place is not a problem  but the bus to get there is to find a little bit hard as the schedule kind of too long to wait

BLUEWATER IMAGING, Scarborough location is extremely clean and does have excellent environment. I did have excellent experience over there on my last visit on Tuesday last week. All the staffs are superb been in their behaviour. Specially the lady who does the ultrasound, cannot remember the name is extremely cordial. I highly recommend the clinic.

Worst place to go to have a ECG arrived at the location at 8am on 4th floor #412 and waited for one hr and they did not register my wife for her appointment. Electrical power went off and power came back and they told her that they had no record of her registering and full power is still not on and the ECG can't be done. Waste of time coming here from Oshawa paid for gas and parking for nothing to come for this appointment. I would not recommend this establishment for any service customer service sucks.

Virtually all medical in 1 building been visiting for years as my Dr. Is here if it's something more than the Dr needs names phone call to other medical specialists nearby for you to see.

name: North York General Hospital – with Dr. Neil Levitsky
Department of Physical Therapy, University of Toronto
address: 160–500 University Avenue, Toronto, ON M5G 1V7, Canada

name: Studio Athletica
address: 55 University Ave M002, Toronto, ON M5J 2H7
description: Amazing service by the doctors, and the fragrance in the air is just amazing

name: Creative Therapy Associates
description:
My child recently just started accessing therapy (SLO, OT and ABA) at Creative. She has tried other places and this is the only one she’s wanted to go and not leave. My only regret is waiting so long to start here.

My child loves going here. The team and centre are so welcoming. I cannot say enough good things about Creative. Highly recommend going here.

Very expensive. They keep increasing the price with their clients. The SLP will charge you for indirect services by saying you will not be charged but when you see the bill they will charge you extra. Not happy with their billing service, very expensive!

name: College of Occupational Therapists of Ontario
address: 20 Bay Street, Suite 900, PO Box 78, Toronto, ON M5J 2N8

name: Humber River Hospital
description:
My experience at Humber River Health has been great and quite impressive so far from the begining when I came to ER, admitted to the hospital for 5 days during Xmas because I had severe appendicitis, I was very well taken care off and treated properly. I am now under followup by Dr Sohi - he is very friendly and explains clearly all my questions.
I am very grateful for everyone - Doctors and The nursing staffs, Food service, cleaners as they were extremely responsible, supportive.
The hospital is clean, modern. I found out that this is the first fully digital hospital in North America.
Impatient room is very nice and well equiped.
There are everything you need like Starbucks, Tim Hortons, RBC, The food court is huge.
Overall Humber River Health deserves 5*

I had a very positive and completely satisfying experience with my C-section delivery at Humber River Hospital in Toronto. From the pre-delivery visit two days before my surgery to discharge, everything was well-organized, thorough, and highly professional.

The operating room staff and the anesthesiologist were calm, kind, and very professional, which made my C-section a stress-free experience. My delivery was performed by Dr. Peyman Mazidi, an excellent, skilled, and trustworthy physician who clearly explained everything and followed up before, during, and after the surgery, making me feel safe and reassured at all times.

I was also under the care of Dr. Ali Tarshizi, an endocrinologist, during my gestational diabetes, and his care was outstanding. He was very attentive and even visited me in my room the day after surgery to check on my condition, which I truly appreciated.

The nursing staff were extremely responsible, supportive, and attentive to both mother and baby, and the room cleanliness was excellent throughout my stay.

Overall, my delivery experience at Humber River Hospital with this outstanding medical and nursing team was one of the calmest and best experiences of my life. I highly recommend this hospital and its care team to all expecting mothers.

If I went solely based off of today’s experience I would have given 5 stars. The young doctor I had in emergency today was incredible. He listened to my concerns and made me feel heard. I was in and out in under 5 hours.

My experience with a prior doctor I felt dismissed , was there for over 12 hours, then went home with no answers after a very long day. My initial emerg doctor was very thorough but at shift change I felt like that changed. It was very busy so I am empathetic but they need to staff according to the trends in volume. The poor staff are overworked.

This visit saved the rating as I previously had it as 1 star but this doctor today was amazing

The experience with my boy at the children’s inpatient unit was great! The paediatricians and nurses were super helpful and nice, they made our experience less stress despite with everything else we were going through. Everything is super new and they even have a movable tv for your little ones, a true life saver for me. A big thank you from my family!

This hospital has by far been the best hospital I have been in all of Ontario! I'm not sure why it's rated so low but I have rated it five stars and I would do that again. This is my fourth visit here and I have enjoyed every visit. The service is amazing, fast and very efficient. The staff is so friendly and kind. And the food court options are endless!

name: North York General Hospital
description:
My wife gave birth at North York General Hospital, and overall, the team provided decent care during delivery and afterward. The staff were generally attentive and professional, and I really appreciated how well they handled the follow-up checks for both my wife and our newborn daughter they were thorough and reassuring.
That said, there were a few instances of conduct that could have been better (like communication or responsiveness at times), which was a bit disappointing. Still, the facilities are clean and modern, and most of the experience was positive. Thanks to the team for helping bring our little one into the world safely.

Great hospital in an ideal location.  Quality doctors and nurses.  It’s where my daughter was born.  Excellent experience.  Emergency department is also top notch.  Got blood test, urine test, X-Ray, ECG and ultrasound done within 6 hours with assessment from doctor.  Not too bad considering the busy time of the year.  Thank you!

I was admitted through the Emergency Department earlier than my scheduled C-section due to the onset of labor and the presence of a mucus plug. The entire process was smooth, with no long waiting time in the Labor and Delivery unit. The environment was clean, and all the nurses were kind and welcoming.
I would especially like to commend two exceptional nurses who truly made a difference in my delivery journey: Kaye Tolentino from the Operating Room and Recovery Area, and Edalaiza from the Postpartum unit.
During the procedure, my whole body was shaking due to extreme nervousness and fear. Kaye reassured me that I was in good hands, and she truly meant it. While I was receiving spinal anesthesia, she made sure I was covered with a warm blanket and comforted me when I was shaking uncontrollably. She even hugged me like a sister, calmly encouraging me to relax and reminding me that everything would be over quickly. I was close to tears because her words and presence meant so much to me. I do not have family here to support me, and the way she treated me with such compassion and sisterly care gave me the strength I needed to get through the operation. Although the entire operating room team was wonderful, Kaye’s kindness was truly exceptional.
In the Postpartum unit, Edalaiza provided outstanding care. She was very thorough in explaining all the procedures for newborn care, ensuring I fully understood everything—even though I already have experience working as an assistant teacher in a daycare and am a mother of two. She supported me in every aspect of my recovery and was very proactive in helping me heal quickly so I could return home to my children, which was my greatest concern. Her knowledge, attentiveness, and initiative toward every patient truly stood out.
I wholeheartedly commend these two nurses in Labor and Delivery. They went beyond their job descriptions by providing not only excellent medical care but also genuine emotional support, which played a significant role in my physical and emotional healing.

I recently had a total hip replacement surgery at North York General Hospital, and I can’t say enough good things about the entire experience—from the registration to discharge.  As an  employee in healthcare myself, I  know what quality care looks like, and this team delivered it at the highest level.

The surgery went smoothly.  Dr.Dhotar , the anesthesia and  PACU team were exceptional. But what truly stood out was the care on 4W Orthopaedic Unit. The nurse who took care of me, especially Lily, were outstanding—compassionate, attentive, and incredibly skilled at pain management anticipated my needs, explained everything clearly, kept me comfortable during my admission, and always made time to answer questions with patience and warmth.

The allied health team—physical therapist Elisa (with assistant Ben, occupational therapist (Aurora) are phenomenal. They were encouraging yet realistic, pushing me just the right amount to get moving early. Farida (pain specialist) provided clear and easy to follow instruction with regards to managing pain.

From the moment I arrived to the day I left, every staff member I encountered was professional, kind, and dedicated. The hospital environment felt clean, organized, and supportive. Thanks to this amazing team.

I recently visited the Emergency Department at North York General Hospital and had an excellent experience. The staff were professional, attentive, and caring, making a stressful situation much easier to handle.
I want to especially thank Dr. Elisha Targonsky for his exceptional care. He was not only highly knowledgeable and skilled but also incredibly kind and patient, taking the time to explain my condition. His professionalism, calm demeanor, and genuine compassion made a huge difference in my experience.
Overall, North York General Hospital provides excellent care, and I am very grateful for the dedication of Dr. Targonsky and the entire medical team.

name: Birchmount Hospital (Scarborough Health Network)
description:
Seeing a emergency doctor in here is an insane wait. There were only two patients next to me and me, but more staff than patients. I waited for 3.5 hours and didn't even see a doctor or nurse. Canada is really employing too many useless people—inefficient and unwilling to work. They work 15 minutes, then rest for 20 minutes and have coffee. No wonder I saw news reports of patients dying while waiting in the ER because they couldn't see a doctor. I just asked the people next to me, and they usually wait 7-10+ hours. This healthcare system is completely diseased. Why isn't anyone doing anything about it and changing it?

⭐️⭐️⭐️ (3.5 stars)
My grandma was admitted to Birchmount Hospital (formerly Scarborough Grace Hospital) about two months ago after having a stroke.
Overall, our experience was mixed. The midnight shift (in the emergency department) was quite disappointing — the staff seemed inattentive, and when I raised my concerns, I was told they were shorthanded. I understand how tough healthcare can be, but it still felt worrying at the time.
Later, when my grandma was transferred to acute care, I unfortunately found a roach running on her food tray. I reported it right away, and to their credit, the nurse responded quickly — they replaced the tray immediately and had the kitchen prepare fresh food for her.
On the positive side, the nurses in the stroke care unit were really wonderful — kind, patient, and attentive. The geriatric staff, physiotherapists, and doctors were also very professional and caring.
So while there were a few unpleasant moments, there were also many staff who truly showed dedication and compassion. I’d rate this hospital around 3 to 3.5 stars overall.

The hospital was nice and clean. The parking lot is near the north entrance. As soon as I entered, two people came and guided me, and within two to three minutes I was in the MRI center, just like that. The interior was very clean, and the clothes they gave me for the MRI were clean, and the staff knew their job. The problem I have with hospitals is their high parking fees. I don't think it's right at all. I ended up leaving the hospital within an hour and paid twelve dollars for parking.

Nurse Malat in the emergency room (zone D?) was extremely helpful and kind, he made our family feel at ease and comfortable.
Both attendings were wonderful as well, very patient, explained everything very well and made sure we understood everything that was happening.
The team made the patient feel very comfortable, treated them with dignity, and were extremely thorough.
Wait times were not ideal but that is somewhat out of their control.

My mom recently received excellent care at this facility for her pneumonia. The amount of attention and expert attention was impressive. Most of The people were very caring and worked hard to deliver their services promptly and were sensitive to my mom's age and condition. I know that overall the health system is receiving bad reviews, but our experience was quite different at this facility. Our Thanks and appreciation goes to the tireless nurses, PCWs , experts and doctors.

name: Scarborough Health Network - General Hospital
description:
My spouse had day surgery here Nov 28/25. We found the hospital and the staff to be mostly fantastic. The hospital had some construction going on but that didn't affect the care received or the ability to navigate the facility. We live several hours away and to us, having shops inside the hospital, options for food aside from Tim's (although we love Tim Hortons) and space for me to sit comfortably and wait was fabulous!!! I have seen some bad reviews andnsome even calling it a 3rd world hospital, but those are definitely not accurate. If those are your thoughts travel a few hours north and see how lucky you really are!!!

The only issue we had was trying to get him discharged. A disgruntled employee made that process take more than 4 times longer than it should have. Despite being told multiple times by their supervisor to get my spouse ready to go she ignored us. The employee even loudly complained about her job, her hours, and the hospital. Thankfully her supervisor finally stepped in and got him ready herself so we could head out as she recognized the delay was now forcing us to drive in snow storm that could have been avoided if the employee had got us ready when first asked.

Overall a good experience and even if short staffed they do their best to take fantastic care of you.

Despite arriving with a lot of stress, I left this hospital feeling calm and truly grateful.

I was unfortunately delayed by about 45 minutes because I was initially directed to the wrong hospital. The message I received had a very similar hospital name and did not include an address, which caused confusion. Combined with evening traffic, this resulted in my late arrival.

However, the CT scan department staff were incredibly kind, understanding, and highly professional. Despite my delay, they cooperated with me warmly, performed my CT scan without any issues, and even checked on how I was feeling afterward. They said goodbye with genuine care, which meant a lot to me.

The hospital itself was very clean, well-organized, and welcoming. The parking fee was also very reasonable, which helped reduce stress.

What impressed me most was how efficient the process was — my CT results were available to my doctor the next day in the morning!
Overall, this was a very positive experience, and I am truly thankful to the compassionate and professional CT scan team.

Highly recommended.

* **Birchmount Hospital**
* **General Hospital**
* **Centenary Hospital**

These are critical reviews, written respectfully but firmly, reflecting common public concerns about Scarborough Health Network (SHN). You can copy and paste any of these into Google Maps under “Write a Review,” or adjust them to include your personal experience.

---

### 🔴 1-Star Review for SHN Birchmount Hospital

> **Extremely disappointing experience.** The emergency room at Birchmount is one of the most overcrowded I’ve seen, with patients left waiting 6+ hours, sometimes without proper updates or triage. Despite this being a hospital that serves thousands of people, they **closed essential departments like pediatrics and obstetrics**—leaving entire families in Scarborough underserved.
>
> The hospital infrastructure is clearly outdated. Hallways are cramped, equipment looks old, and the environment does not feel clean or modern. It's hard to feel confident in a facility that feels neglected and underfunded.
>
> The staff seem overworked and under-supported. Some are trying their best, but the system is broken. Scarborough deserves better than a facility that operates like an afterthought.
>
> This isn’t a place I’d recommend unless you have no other choice.

---

### 🔴 1-Star Review for SHN General Hospital

> **General Hospital feels like a facility stuck in the past.** The fact that some of the operating rooms are still from the 1950s is shocking. For a hospital serving such a large and diverse community, the physical conditions are unacceptable.
>
> Long ER wait times, overcrowding, and what feels like constant under-staffing make for an experience that’s frustrating at best, dangerous at worst. It's clear that the hospital is overwhelmed—patients are lined up in hallways, and privacy feels non-existent.
>
> SHN constantly launches fundraising campaigns, yet major system issues remain unsolved. It raises the question: where is the support from the province? Why is such a vital hospital treated like an afterthought?
>
> I wouldn't bring a family member here unless it was an emergency and no other option was available.

---

### 🔴 1-Star Review for SHN Centenary Hospital

> **Disorganized, overcrowded, and disappointing.** Centenary Hospital feels overwhelmed every time I’ve had to visit—whether for ER visits or supporting a loved one. The emergency department is chaotic, and communication with patients is minimal to nonexistent.
>
> Staff are trying, but you can tell morale is low and resources are stretched. The care often feels rushed, and it’s easy to feel like just a number in the system. Cleanliness and upkeep are also not at the level a hospital should be.
>
> SHN’s decision to possibly consolidate or close hospital sites (while this one already runs over capacity) shows a real lack of long-term vision for Scarborough's healthcare needs.
>
> Definitely not a facility I would willingly return to unless there were absolutely no other options available.

---

### ⚠️ Optional Ending Line for All Reviews (If You Want to Add It):

> **If you can go elsewhere, do it. Scarborough deserves better.**

---

I understand that many people might have negative experiences, and this is common when people can't respond quickly in desperate emergency situations. However, I had no problems with a recent baby delivery. For those who are pregnant, I would recommend calling in advance to see if the admittance criteria are met. Nights in public areas are quiet and sometimes hallways feel like an empty airport. The maternity ward (Family Birthing Unit) is operational with full service 24 hours a day, and staff are knowledgeable. I would recommend an H-Pass for long term visits for multiple days, as short term parking is expensive.

I had a very positive experience delivering my baby at Scarborough General Hospital. The nurses were amazing, kind, and very supportive throughout my stay. Special thanks to nurses Eman, Anupreet, and Anne Mary for their care, patience, and compassion—they truly made a stressful time feel much easier. Dr. Jia who delivered my baby was also excellent and very reassuring. I’m grateful to the whole team for making my delivery experience positive and comfortable.

name: Mount Sinai Hospital
description:
We just had our baby at this hospital, and the experience was truly outstanding. We don’t have a single complaint—on the contrary, we want to congratulate the entire staff.

From the moment my wife was admitted, the care was more than kind. The nurses were extremely attentive and thoughtful, all the way through delivery. We had multiple doctors and nurses supporting us during labor, as well as a team of specialists ready for when our baby was born. Everything went perfectly.

Our delivery room was very clean and fully equipped to keep us comfortable. After our baby was born, we were moved to a semi-private room, and everything was organized and in perfect condition. The instructions were very clear, and they provide excellent information to take home—especially helpful for first-time parents.

For new parents, the parking in front of the hospital on Murray Street is very convenient, and the food area on the ground floor makes it easy to grab something to eat during those waiting hours.

100/100 in every aspect. Congratulations, and keep up the great work! In the end, it truly feels like the team working there genuinely enjoys what they do—and that makes all the difference.

I absolutely loved my entire birthing experience at Mount Sinai Toronto. From my OB prenatal appointments with Dr. John Snelgrove to the delivery and postpartum care, everything was beyond amazing. They didn't send me home as my water-broke. The nurses and staff were so warm, kind, and genuinely caring — it truly felt like everyone was invested in making sure both me and my baby were doing well. Nothing ever felt rushed or delayed; everything happened so naturally, at the right time, with the right experts guiding us.

They not only took care of all the postpartum essentials me and my baby but also made sure we learned so much during the postpartum phase. The number of specialists who checked in and the level of attention we received were simply incredible. I’m so grateful to every doctor, nurse, and staff member who made this experience so positive, comforting, and memorable. It’s something I’ll cherish forever. ❤️

I had my baby here. Extremely good obgyns, doctors and nurses. I felt very supported and listened to my entire pregnancy. Instead of going to ER they have a separate pregnancy ward and they always support and take care of you no matter what scare you may be having. I stayed for 5 days for my entire induction and delivery and everything. I am grateful to everyone.

I had an incredible experience delivering my baby at this hospital. I cannot thank Dr. Marina Vainder and my wonderful nurses, Valentina and Essi, enough for their exceptional care. Dr. Vainder was professional, calm, and made me feel safe throughout the entire process. Valentina and Essi were absolute angels—so attentive, kind, and supportive during every step of my labor and recovery. They made one of the most important days of my life so much easier. Highly recommend this amazing team!"

We gave birth at Mount Sinai Hospital in Toronto and had an amazing experience on both the 15th floor labour and delivery unit and the 16th floor mother and baby unit. From the moment we arrived, we felt supported, safe, and genuinely cared for.

During labour and delivery, we were lucky to have Nurse Sophie with us. She was incredible. So kind, calm, and encouraging throughout the entire process. She made sure we were comfortable, explained everything clearly, and was just an amazing presence to have by our side. We’ll never forget how much of a difference she made during such an important moment in our lives.

The facilities were great. The birthing suite was clean and well set up, with a big window and lots of space to move around. After delivery, we were moved up to the 16th floor where the care continued to be excellent. The nurses there were just as kind and helpful, checking in often and supporting us as new parents.

Mount Sinai truly lived up to its reputation as one of the best places to give birth in Ontario. Everyone we met was professional, compassionate, and made us feel like we were in good hands. We are so thankful for the care we received, and we’ll always remember it as a special part of our journey.

name: Toronto Western Hospital
address: 399 Bathurst Street, 1st Floor, Fell Pavilion, M5T 2S8
description:
I was in at this hospital today at around 2:30 pm and I was very impressed and grateful for how they handled me and how they operate! I had a severe asthma attack and brought myself in to the hospital after not having able to breath properly for many hours. Once I entered, the front desk was extremely helpful in checking me in, to triage, tests, puffers, amazing nurses, doctor and had an x-ray. All of this happen in about 4 hours and I was extremely grateful to have come out with medicine, feeling much better and they practically saved me. I appreciate all the kindness and support from each staff and how they made me feel so taken care of. I thank you all for the 10/10 hospital experience.

The best experience you could have at a hospital. Hands down. I went into emergency. And ended up being admitted for surgery. My experience was nothing short of amazing. The nurses in the ER are kind and genuine. The ER doctor cared about everything and showed great compassion. The neurosurgery team came into the ER to let me know I would be admitted and explained everything and took great care. The surgery was flawless and left me with life altering results to feeling back to myself again. Dr. massicotte was the surgeon and he is very good at his job. The post op team was great. After my surgery I had a student nurse who will make an incredible nurse one day as she was meant to do that job. They never gave me a hard time and always checked in and anytime I asked for something they made sure they advocated for me. The physio team was diligent and extremely helpful. I will forever be greatful to Toronto Western Hospital for giving me my life back. I'm from our of town and was advised to go here because my local hospital is awful, and Toronto really put that in perspective for me. All I can say is thank you, this place knows how to treat people.

I had surgery a week ago. I was checked in with efficiency, kindness and professionalism. I hope I get the names right throughout but D-Rhonda, Sarah were great pre-op admissions and getting me to surgical team on time and being positive. In the next area, the folks who took my blood were friendly and warm, we joked around a little bit really relaxing me. Off to surgical O.R. Everyone in the O.R. Introduced themselves and told me what they do, very friendly and professional. Totally kept me calm and relax. Anesthesia administered and I woke up in Recovery. Feeling ok, no nausea, no pain yet. Jenna worked with me, waking me up and then dealing with my EKG monitor, with me throwing p waves and being AFib. Was in recovery a little longer then expected, due to my issues though it was normal. I was finally taken to my room where I met my care nurse Alexandra. Super nice, polite, friendly, very welcoming and giving me explanations for all my questions. The night shift nurse Rosie came on at 7:30pm and was just as nice. I was moved at midnight to the cardiac care floor as my AFib wasn’t stopping. My nurses on cardiac care were Yuma and Tanzen, again both very friendly and professional as they went through my vitals and explained my questions to me. These folks were with me until discharge later that day (5:00pm) was sent home was sorry to go as these people made my surgery and stay all the better. Was very impressed. Dr. Okrainec was excellent for my whole stay as well as the two other Doctors on his team (I forget their names, one female and one male, sorry) overall a solid 5 stars, if I had to say anything negative, I didn’t really want to walk through the food court on the way out but it’s how I had to leave to get to the parking. 😬😂.
Best care anywhere!

Had a terrible experience here when my back was out. (Paramedics were very kind). Sat in the ambulance bay for a couple hours, finally got a semi secluded corner with curtain. Doctor came a few hours later, barely checked me and talked to me with contempt. No tests run, had to practically beg for pain relief and was treated like I was drug seeking. And then after super flippant treatment or lack there of had to try and get myself a cab while I can’t walk. Nurses just watched as my partner and I struggled and cried to put me in a wheel chair - one that another patient had offered me. Was in excruciating pain and ended up being bed ridden for a full month.

* additionally, heard the nurses talking to an elderly man who was crying for his mother he was in so much pain. Heard them say to him “who are you calling for? Your mom?  Well she’s not here”. Told him his pain was just anxiety and down played it.

If you’re able to choose to go to another hospital, especially in an emergency, DO IT.

My boyfriend injured his knee and was rushed to the emergency room. He was kept there overnight while they ran tests and we were told he would receive an MRI. The next morning, we were informed there was no availability and he was sent home in a brace with crutches. We were told the hospital would contact us that same week to schedule the MRI. We never received a call. When we followed up ourselves, we were told the MRI request had been cancelled when he was discharged from the ER — something no one informed us about. We were then told the earliest available appointment was in May, despite the injury happening in January. After pushing for an earlier appointment, we were finally able to get the MRI moved up. Unfortunately, the scan did not even cover the correct area of his leg. Now he is left waiting yet again, still in significant pain and without clear answers about his injury. This experience has been incredibly frustrating and disappointing. The lack of communication, delays in care, and errors in imaging have prolonged his pain and recovery. We expected better coordination and follow-through, especially given the severity of his injury.

name: Women’s College Hospital
description:
I am a 22-year-old patient who waited nearly two years to see Dr. Lemos. Unfortunately, my experience at this women’s hospital was deeply distressing and traumatic.

At my first appointment after 1.5 years, I was scheduled for a urodynamic study. I arrived early and on time and clearly explained to the nurse that I have pelvic floor dysfunction and cannot hold my bladder for long periods. I asked multiple times how long the wait would be and was told approximately 30 minutes. When I explained I could not hold my bladder that long, the nurse told me to empty my bladder.

Shortly after using the bathroom (as instructed), I was called in, only to be told that my bladder now needed to refill — resulting in an additional 1.5-hour delay. I was later blamed for using the bathroom despite having asked for clarification multiple times and following staff instructions.

During the procedure, the nurse assisting me was kind, compassionate, and did her best to support me through what was the most painful medical experience I have ever had. Due to the severity of the pain, dizziness, and shortness of breath, I was unable to complete the test.

When Dr. Lemos entered the room, he did not introduce himself and responded to my distress dismissively, stating that many women complete the test and implying that I should be able to as well. When I asked what to expect next, I was told that nothing could be done since the test was incomplete and that any further testing would be inconclusive.

For two months following this appointment, I experienced internal bleeding, severe pain with urination, and was unable to engage in sexual activity due to the trauma and physical pain.

After hearing nothing from the hospital, Dr. Lemos’ assistant later contacted me regarding an ultrasound referral that I had never received. I explained my experience honestly and respectfully. Shortly afterward, I was informed that Dr. Lemos would no longer provide me care because he was “uncomfortable” with how I described what happened.

Since then, both my family doctor and I have been requesting my medical records for nearly a month without success.

I am sharing this not to cause conflict, but to warn other young women — especially those dealing with suspected endometriosis or chronic pelvic pain — to seek providers who show compassion, accountability, and trauma-informed care. No patient should be made to feel like a lab subject or dismissed during moments of extreme vulnerability.

had top surgery with Dr. Armstrong yesterday and she is incredible!!
her and the whole team were so incredibly kind and explained everything as it was happening so i knew exactly what was going on.
other doctors were involved, like Dr. Xu and Dr. Gupta who were also incredible.
I got to talk to some med students who I believe also were in the OR for my surgery- they will make great doctors, they were so kind and compassionate.
I am so glad i got to have Dr. Armstrong as my surgeon and would recommend her as a great option for anyone wanting to get on a list for top surgery.

Had surgery with them today and honestly the entire team on the 8th floor was amazing. Receptionists, nurses, anesthesiologist. Everyone was kind and efficient…and big shout out to Dr.İm for being a real one! Am rarely a happy camper but this experience was 10/10.

I was suggested to go to this hospital because I was unable to go back to Montreal to see my surgeon about post-op care.

When I called to ask if anyone was able to remove my drains (while I was in tremendous pain from trying to get them removed earlier that day by my family doctor who couldn’t complete the task), the lady on the phone proceeded to scold me for thinking “just anyone” could do it and literally told me to go back to Montreal. She seemed offended I got surgery elsewhere (I couldn’t afford it in Toronto) and told me they could get sued for removing my drains. I was told by both my family doctor and my surgeon that the hospital would be able to do it as it’s a pretty standard thing they do. Apparently it is not and they get pretty pissed off about it if you call. She kept going on and on as if I was the dumbest person alive- all I needed was a yes or no to if they could see me or not.

I ended up getting care elsewhere with no questions asked. As other reviews previously mentioned, it’s worth it to go to another place that is sympathetic and respects you, even if they may not be able to help.

My experience at Woman's Collage Hospital at the AACU unit was 5⭐  From the receptionist's the nursing staff and Drs. Everyone was very friendly has smiles on their faces and took their time with you. I was there twice. Both times same wonderful experience. I had the same nurse both times. Her name is Elsie. She was so caring. My husband and her had some nice chats ☺️ Another bonus is the hospital has underground parking. It's pricey but saves you from driving around finding parking. The reason for the 4 star is the drive to Downton is so congested.

name: Albany Medical Clinic
description:
I want to take a moment to recognize the incredible administrative staff member at this clinic. She is exceptionally responsive, organized, and consistently goes above and beyond. During my visits, I witnessed her handle numerous difficult and disrespectful interactions with calm, grace, and unwavering professionalism — something that truly impressed me.

As a parent of two small children, I am beyond grateful for the particular care, patience, and kindness she showed us during our time at the clinic. Her support meant more than words can describe during a stressful moment. She is truly a gem, and this clinic is very lucky to have her.

My first time at the clinic. Front desk was professional, and informative. I waited an hour not bad, I was prepared to wait for more time since it's always very busy clinic. Dr. Lauren addressed my concerns with empathy and understanding professionalism. Thank you. 🙏🏽👩‍⚕️ #FirstVisit #ProfessionalService #EmpathyAndUnderstanding
Keep up the good work 👏
Many blessings.

For a walk clinic, these folks get the highest rating. I waited almost 15 minutes on hold on a Saturday morning but they have an interactive recording telling you where you are in line.  I got an appointment that afternoon.  Dr. Haddad was very efficient and thorough. I explained that I had a flight in three days and needed to know if I was ok to travel with an eye injury.  She processed the X-ray request for Monday morning and my GP had results hours later.  There was also a follow-up phone call from Albany that evening. The staff were all efficient and friendly.   Walk in clinics by definition can be frustrating experiences but Albany stood out as how this sometimes problematic  part of Canadian health care can deliver the necessary services in a timely fashion.

When the city of Toronto has been under dangerous freezing cold conditions over a span of 22 straight days, I am deeply grateful for a strong presence of Albany Medical Clinic in my neighbourhood of Broadview and Danforth.

As a pregnant patient and new immigrant, I have the utmost confidence in this clinic where the family physicians, specialists and support staff have been looking after me very well. I owe them a big thank-you.

Please continue the immaculate work, Albany Medical Clinic!

I see some terrible reviews here but have had nothing but at least bare-minimum “good” experiences over the years. My family doctor has been here since I’ve been a child and I am getting turned over to a new one as he is retiring. They’re nice to me at the front desk and I see that they are nice and helpful to others as well. I’ve been to the walk-in over the years and also had bloodwork and other things done, and been dealt with in a professional and helpful manner. It is nearly impossible to get a short notice appointment but I blame the healthcare system for this.

This 3- story clinic is basically a one- stop shop for most healthcare needs… barring things like surgeries.

This program is fantastic.everyone has been so amazing with me as I recover.very caring and helpful.Iam blessed too be in this program.thank you too everyone involved.I highly recommend closing the gap team .It's nice too know my recovery is their main goal.

name: Parkinson Society Southwestern Ontario
description:
I have been writing poems about my journey with Parkinson's Disease. I contacted the association to share my story.  In March a gentleman came to see me and seem to be genuinely touched by my poems.  I turned in a copy of  my poems and waited  with  excitement to see what came next..
After a few weeks I contacted them see what was happening in April had been busy so not much could be done yet. I did receive a phone call recently that they were sending my poems back. I asked what was happening and I was given a very vague answer.

Very informative and helpful.  An information package was sent out quickly.  If you are developing tremors and think you may have Parkinson’s, don’t delay. It’s better to know what you are up against . They will direct you to the nearest support group in your area .Posted by Preston .

name: LIFE*SPIN
description:
Hi!
I recently went to LifeSpins 'free store'  my first time going there was yesterday. When I got there, I had no direction on what your supposed to do. There is a man sitting in a chair taking people's names and writing them down, a lady poked her head out of the store door and called a number. I didn't want to loose my place and people were coming so I asked the man, where do I get the ticket with the number to be called. He didn't saying anything. Thinking he's hard of hearing I say it again, still not response.  So I go to the front door to see if I can read anything that tells me what to do. There are 3 steps and I step up the first one and he grabs my arm and yells at me ' you cant do that' i said back ' I wasn't going inside' he yells at me again, so I yelled back like a little kid. It really hurt my feeling and for him to grab me like that triggered me big time. So I got everything filled out and got my number and cried while I waited. For some reason the interaction between us has triggered me big time. I finally make it in the door after stand in line outside. I got in and tell the lady that's helping me what happened and how it upset me. She said you dont talk to me you talk to her, shes the manager.  So I go to the 'manager' told her what happened, and she says he probably didn't yell at you, totally disregarding thw fact he put his hands on me. I said no he yelled and it upset me. She sluffed it off again. So I asked do u have food here, she says no she only has clothes. So I said thank you again left.
I've been to the other side of life spin where they actually have paid staff and offer resources,  they are very friendly and kind.  I cant say much about the ' freestore' life spins putting on, but I tell you this much. Thats my last time going!  Hope this helps someone with there choice to go.

Staff is beyond rude and completely ungrateful for donations. Diapers and toiletries, clothing from their specific list we asked what they needed and they knew we were doing a collection. Show up lady complained the whole time was so rude to us they had no room for the stuff and didn't know we were coming.  Even though we booked an appointment and asked if they wanted us to do the collection. The people on the street waiting to shop were kinder and more helpful. Even they were taken back by the behavior of the staff, they told us to donate elsewhere next time. Good advice.  Also got screamed at for parking in a spot makes life spin stating it was for the "executive director" and she would park behind me and block me in all day when she arrived.  Like seriously was just looking for staff to assist me bringing in 2 literal car loads of items.

This is the worst place in london ontario they say the help people when they don't  i register for help with christmas as this is the only place that helps with older kids and what help did i get was none I email them and message them on Facebook for them to block me off Facebook because I ask a question  and this is what I asked  was .Are they still delivering hampers as i signed up and I still not got a hamper for my kids 😕 got my confirmation email that I was approved  but nothing else. When they made a post about being closed  so I guess that's worng as that's all I ask the did email me back saying that I got a food box  yes I did get a food box from On The Move Organics  in November  the email i got for that said it was for the The Community Food Box Project? Not the christmas program and now thier telling that it was the christmas program. Relly any one who knows thier two different programs and if I thought that they think vegetables would last two months for christmas dinner .
No where in the email says it was for christmas program either
Attached picture of the email  that shows  no where it's saying it's part of the christmas program

Life spin first of all is a wonderful helpful place to the general public. The staff are nothing but amazing and help in anyway they can. I have needed help with back pack school drive. Providing backpacks and school supplies. Also the free store is also another great outlet available when funds are tight and you need help for decent and nice clothing plus other items too. Overall I am thankful to all the staff at Life spin for all they do!! Keep it up!! Thanks so much! 💞

Life spin and it's staff are amazing they are very kind and helpful and they are there for people and families in need. They helped my family at Christmas and their sponsors are just amazing thank you life spin and your sponsors for making our Christmas a great one!  I hope you know how much you truly mean to families such as mine. By no means do they have to or are required to help you but they do and for that we are truly Grateful so thank you for your kind gesture and helping hand!!

This was done in plain sight of other staff and community. The leadership is absolutely vacant, out to lunch. The community member is still waiting for an official apology from the organization. This is the only location for Psychiatric help and as a Two-Spirit Ojibwe woman, the demand for female psychiatrists are high as I’m aware there’s only ONE available.

This place offers Indigenous Peoples a place to get all health services here. From seeing a doctor for referills or a dentist when needing work done on your teeth. They will help you with addictions counseling as well as therapy. Social workers on site. Doctors on site as well as helping with homeless outreach. If you need help with putting g your life back together they can help. Dont hesitateto deop in and ask for help.

Great place of healing! Thanks to all the team members that work tirelessly to assist the community. Pete, you are so special! Thanks for always making the bookings so seamless Shelly! Great job everyone.

A very warm welcome with respect and kindness from lining up to leaving, from each and everyone you come in contact with , wow if all places could be like this it would be a wonderful place to live, so simple with no effort just love and peace and joy.

name: Mamaway Wiidokdaadwin (Orillia Clinic)
description:

Basic human respect and compassion is what we all need and the individuals in this clinic including NP Cathy Tumber provide just that. I felt so understood and know I'm in good hands now. I have never in my life felt this way in Healthcare for myself and kids. I always had my guard up waiting for the comments. Thank you so much for the much needed smudge after weeks of illness. Niawenhkowa big thank you

So nice to finally feel heard and seen by a primary care provider! So refreshing that all of the staff here are so friendly and also knowledgeable.  And the opportunity for traditional medicine is awesome.

I was invited to an event on March 30. I was told to send an email, which I did to confirm my attendance and my participation on Mar 19.  I was just informed today that my registration was missed so I was officially uninvited today.. I have never been treated with such unprofessionalism and disrespect before in my entire 60 years.

I cherish my experiences with mamaway. It's a lovely organization with beautiful people and teachings that assist me on my healing journey.

name: CHEO Clinic for Augmentative Communication
description:
When I first arrived in Canada as a newcomer, I spent nearly two months with my daughter at CHEO. It was a challenging time for us, but the experience at CHEO made a huge difference.
Dr. Gardin closely monitored my daughter’s condition, providing clear explanations and answering all our questions with patience. The surgery was performed successfully by Dr. Maharajh, whose professionalism and care gave us great confidence throughout the process.
The nurses were incredibly kind and gentle with my daughter, helping to ease her stress and mine as well. Throughout our stay, I felt supported, respected, and cared for—not only medically, but emotionally as a parent in a new country.
I am very grateful to Dr. Gardin, Dr. Maharajh, and the entire CHEO team for their compassion and expertise.

We were sent to CHEO ER with a referral from our doctor today for an infant ultrasound (our baby is 5.5 months, we suspect a milk allergy, our GP wanted to rule out intussusception). The security guard that spoke with us upon arrival seemed a little too happy to inform us of the potential 20-hour waiting time ahead of us. Due to it being flu season, only one parent was allowed to stay in the waiting area with our baby. I left my wife with our son and headed for the place I was allowed to wait at the opposite end of the hospital. Beyond the initial waiting area, every hallway I walked through was lined with more parents and their children, waiting. It was a real shock to see. I returned shortly thereafter to my wife and son and we decided to head to the comforts of home rather than risk gathering a different illness from the cramped ER. The whole experience left me feeling helpless as a new parent. What a sad state for a children’s urgent care facility.

Edit: I have to revise my review following having to return to CHEO four days later and finally getting beyond the emergency room after an arduous 8-hour wait post-initial-triage. The nurses and doctors are all wonderful, hard-working individuals that have been so kind and loving. No one working the floor is at fault for the lack of support hospitals are receiving from both provincial and federal governments. If your child is sick, these people will take care of them, you just have to get through that first challenging hump. If you’re reading this, I wish you and your family well.

I am very disappointed with my experience at CHEO ER, especially with the registration staff.

I brought my young son to the ER, where he was assessed by a doctor and suspected to have strep throat. The doctor clearly told me a throat sample would be sent to the lab, results would take 1–2 days, and I would receive a phone call with a prescription if the result was positive.

The result came back positive on MyChart, yet no one ever called me.

During this time, my son became extremely unwell. He did not eat or drink for two full days and did not urinate the entire day before I returned to the ER. Concerned and desperate, I went back to the ER only to get the prescription so my child could start treatment.

At registration, instead of receiving help or concern, the staff member was very rude, dismissive, and even laughed. When I explained that my son hadn’t been eating, drinking, or peeing, she showed no care or urgency at all. She repeatedly said, “They will call you when they call you. Maybe tomorrow, maybe after tomorrow.”

I was told that my only options were:
•	Wait at home for a call that never came, or
•	Sit in the ER for another minimum of 8 hours just to see a doctor again only for a prescription.

This was incredibly frustrating and unsafe.

To make matters worse, I am pregnant and due next month, yet I was not allowed to have my husband accompany me inside to help manage my sick child. Handling a very ill child alone, while pregnant, with no compassion or flexibility shown by staff, was overwhelming.

The lack of communication, empathy, and basic professionalism at registration was shocking. No parent should be treated this way—especially when their child is clearly unwell and at risk of dehydration.

CHEO is supposed to be a children’s hospital that supports families. Unfortunately, this experience showed the complete opposite.

I had a terrible experience just bringing my sister over to be taken care of by them. She had appendicitis and they did a process on her (waaay after the initial day they said they would begin)  not only did they starve my 12 year old sister for days before her surgery even though they began on her a couple days after they were supposed to begin. They told my younger sister that this is a super safe and regular procedure. A few weeks after this surgery my sister is still feeling massive pain in her appendix and it’s a different pain this time. She hasn’t been to school in almost a week. I just came to find out that this CHEO location where it’s supposed to be filled with professionals and people who don’t half ass their job botched my sisters appendicitis surgery. Some kind of professionals you guys are, terrible punctuality, starving children and NOW BOTCHING “safe” procedures? I am all kinds of disgusting by the way these people act and do Thor job. Is Canadas healthcare this much of a joke? The staff at this location  need to be held accountable for their actions.

I must say I came prepared for an all-nighter (phone chargers and all) when I came in with my Son for a leg pain on Weekend at approx 1:00 PM. Within three hours, he was registered, examined, and seen by a Doctor, and before we knew it, we were done. Staff was super friendly, knowledgable and efficient. Keep up the good work 👍🏻.

There is a very bad doctor named Sue. She is violent in dealing . She abused  and I have witnessed with my kids . They write a false report and close the file. It is a really bad place just your lose time and not take care of your child. I don’t recommend

Very Negative experience with this Niagara Children's Centre School Authority, and especially with its current principle (hopefully not for so long) Mrs. Hodson. She will be fakenly smiling into your face but has no sincere compassion and understanding of your situation. As a parent, you would be feeling like you have placed your kid into a special institution w/o any access to him/her and will be refused to see where/how he exercising there, and how he/her being feed and how (!) gets injuries there. There is no real help to your child but they will be blaming you, the parent, for everything that they cannot do there themself. On a top of everything, local OT, occupation therapist (Anne Kenney, there now), would orchestrate a claim to the social services based on false information with the support of the current principle. And, eventually, you very tired of their manipulations and lies, would take your child out this non-for-profit organization that only cares about Donations. Thus, think about your kid safety first and do not donate to the principle & school staffs salaries.

Just want to say how wonderful Erica is . I had my son in at the Center today for an assessment and honestly , it was the most beneficial appt I have had yet . We are looking forward to our next appointment!

Thanks Erica !

name: Pathways Health Centre for Children Augmentative Communication Service
description:
Every single staff member we have encountered — from reception, to the hearing specialists, to the doctors completing the autism assessment, to the occupational therapists, social workers, and the staff who go directly into daycares to support our children — every one of them has been kind, patient, and genuinely compassionate.
Not once have we felt dismissed. Not once have we felt like “just another family.” Every person we’ve met has had the biggest heart and has treated our daughter with care and respect.
I know Pathways is appreciated in our community, but I truly don’t think they get enough credit for the life-changing work they do every day. We are so incredibly lucky to have them.
And whoever is responsible for hiring there deserves recognition too — because they clearly know how to choose truly exceptional human beings.
Thank you for everything you do for families like ours. We are beyond grateful.

I am so grateful for the help they have provided for our family.  The staff are incredible and dedicated to helping our children.  We are so lucky to have Pathways in our Community!

I have called multiple times and even got my doctor te refer my daughter here however I have left messages. And still have had no one call me back.. it’s frustrating because I need help with my two year olds speech and have heard great things but haven’t had anyone respond 😒

Awesome people, awesome programs. Thank goodness for public support. This organization is top shelf!!!!

pretty good

name: TVCC Augmentative Communication Services
address: 779 Base Line Road East London, ON, N6C 5Y6
description:
Back over 10 years, this great organization gave a significant and effective therapy for my autistic daughter. Just yesterday I went through some of psychological assessments, sheets and more documents done that time and many memories came in. My daughter and I were very lucky to receive a great help from angels, professionals, and hard working therapists from TVCC. The big teamwork they have is amazing. Much respect and prayers to you from Windsor 🙏

If your child is a client of TVCC they are in very good hands in my opinion. My daughter has been a client of TVCC for the past 2 years & they have been nothing but helpful to her. They are also really great with educating the parent with strategies to help your child progress. I am very thankful for TVCC & my daughter absolutely love's going there & when a child enjoys themselves somewhere & looks forward to going there that it self says alot. They have been & still are a great support to my daughter & myself. Much thanks TVCC I think it's amazing you have been supporting children for 70 years & I am certain many more to come. Update my daughter has been taking part in the Entry To School program. I think ETS is a good program however my daughter was attacked by a peer & I can't say I am satisfied with how this was addressed. My daughter was bit by a peer one day, full set of teeth marks in her arm. The 2nd & 3rd day there is bruising on her arm. I wanted to know how the child's aggressive behavior towards my daughter was addressed & I really feel like I wasn't given a clear explanation or the person I was speaking with didn't know how to give me a clear explanation & I knew that the diagnosis of autism would be used as an excuse which I don't find acceptable as my daughter is also on the spectrum which is why she is in the program. When I had said that I didn't want my daughter exposed to violence the response I got is that was my choice which I don't feel is appropriate as my daughter has the right to take part in this program & not have to worry about being aggressively attacked because that is how the other peer "communicates" which is unacceptable autistic or not. I personally will not allow my daughter to use autism as an excuse for any type of inappropriate behavior & I will also not allow the label of autism to make her think or feel that she is incapable of anything. Violence should not be tolerated period & when a parent expresses that they don't want their child exposed to violent behavior the well that is your choice is not the right response. I am thankful that my daughter wasn't traumatized by this act of violence however I very strongly feel that it wasn't addressed appropriately which is very unfortunate because violence is never ok & shouldn't be considered a way of communicating!. I really hope that they do better when dealing with children that are aggressive because not all children with autism are aggressive & every child should feel safe in a classroom environment. I still am not clear about how this happened. I wanted to know how the peer that bit my daughter how her aggressive behavior was addressed but as I said I really didn't receive any clear answers & I don't think this is acceptable. I still like TVCC & my daughter is still happy to attend ETS but this is something that shouldn't have happened or been excused. It's like they just want to sweep it under the rug but the point is my child was attacked in a group of 10 children where there are 4 adults running the program which I think is more than enough & makes it even harder to swallow that my daughter had to be subjected to another peers violence & I guess it doesn't matter that I don't want my daughter exposed to violence as I was told that was my choice which in my opinion is not the right response.

Does anyone know of a private speech therapist in London that they can recommend? Mommy I need your help please

I would give a 0 if I could. My daughter needed Pt, Ot & St. The Pt & Ot have multiple complaints about them from various families in need in the community. They have never been reprimanded and continue to go out and "work"with families. The Social Worker has said "they are very limited in the help they can provid". We've been with Tvcc for over a year now and have recieved no help at all. Our tax dollars should not be paying for this program. I think my next steps is to call the governing authority to stop these therapists from harming children. This is not a Child Lead program rather whatvisvin the best interest of the therapists. This programs needs to be reconstructed from scratch. I've very disheartening that this organization feels they have the right to take advance of families & children in need of their services. Shame on you Tvcc, to allow this type of behavior to continue when you are fully aware of it

name: SSM Open Minds Core Services Centre
ARCH Disability Law Centre
address: Bloor St. E., Ste. 110, Toronto, Ontario M4W 3R5
description:
I have attempted to get in touch with ARCH Disability Legal Aid on multiple occasions, calling several times, leaving voicemails, and sending emails. Unfortunately, I have not received any reply at all. The lack of communication is extremely frustrating and concerning, especially for an organization that provides essential support to people with disabilities. Better responsiveness is urgently needed.

December 15 - Update to the owner's response 1 week ago: Maybe you'd have record if so much time didn't go by from all of the red tape you make me jump through. Pull yourselves together. Deeply frustrating, especially being someone who deals with a brain injury. SHAME ON YOU.

Update to my review:  5 weeks and still no callback, no voicemail, no email. Cold move.  Now watch, they'll take the time to defend this review like they did the last one, but not grant the respect of closure. What a disappointment. SHAME

I cannot express enough how much ARCH and Gabriel has stood in the gap and fought to help my grandson!
Such compassion and strong representation.
We are very grateful!
Thank you to ARCH Disability Law Center and to our fantastic counsel from Gabriel!
I hope I never have to go through these disability barriers against my little guy again however if ever needing representation for disability needs arise again,
I recommend ARCH Disability Law Center.
Deborah

Like all legal aid lawyers in Ontario these are of no exception, the worst lawyers. Don't waist your time. I swear to God, ARCH have the, newest, least experienced, thick in the head lawyers that can't get work anywhere else. What a disappointment!

Lawyer Gabriel Reznick is professional, intelligent and highly dedicated to helping. After years of trying on my own to get a particular public service set up for my family member (but getting stonewalled by the other legal rep), we finally got the victory with Arch Disability.
Gabriel—thank you so much for pushing through the stonewall.
